data.body:
PASS 1 (A1 anti-slop/over-engineering/NIH) — Cluster 1: clean, no findings.

Verified against the codebase:
- Rule-of-Three: the `_store/canonical.py` seam already exists with 6 positional consumers (event_append, _commands/{txn,delete,compact}, rebar_reconciler/{inbound_translate,batch_dispatch}) and the plan migrates 4 *real* additional duplicate sites (signing.py:189, schema.py:396, conflict_resolver.py:218, mutation.py:106). This is de-duplication of confirmed existing copies, not premature abstraction — there is even a prior janitor recommendation for exactly this consolidation (.joe-janitor/report-2026-06-24.md:62).
- NIH: PASS — the change DELETES 4 hand-rolled json.dumps+sha256 reimplementations onto one tested seam; it builds nothing new (stdlib json only).
- Config-surface: the two new keyword-only params are each load-bearing for byte-preservation with cited consumers — ascii_only=True for mutation.py (verified ensure_ascii=True at line 106) and conflict_resolver.py (verified line 218 omits ensure_ascii → stdlib default True); default=str for conflict_resolver.py (verified line 218). Not gratuitous toggles.
- Import boundary sanity-checked: reconciler modules inbound_translate.py:218 / batch_dispatch.py:211 already do `from rebar._store.canonical import canonical_str`, so the new conflict_resolver/mutation imports of the same seam follow established precedent under the subprocess PYTHONPATH (docs/architecture.md ~L100).
- No golden-hammer / cargo-cult / resume-driven / premature-optimization patterns.

Affirmations: Rule-of-Three satisfied; YAGNI (each param serves a current byte-preservation done-definition); no NIH (removes duplication, rebuilds nothing); no config-surface proliferation; clean on the full DSO anti-pattern set.
